⚡️ 🇮🇱 🇮🇷 The Israeli Air Force, acting on military intelligence, struck a petrochemical complex in Mahshahr, south‑western Iran, which was responsible for producing chemicals for the "regime’s weapons." The site was one of two central facilities for manufacturing materials used in explosives, ballistic missiles and other arms. Damage to this infrastructure will disrupt Iran’s ability to produce weapons designed to target Israel and other Middle Eastern countries. The strike is part of the campaign to deepen the impact on Iran’s military foundations. @wfwitness

After destroying 70% of Iran’s steel production capacity, which provides raw materials for weapons, Israel today struck Iranian petrochemical plants. “These two things are the regime’s money machine to fuel its war of terror against us and against the entire world,” he said. “We will continue to pound them.” @wfwitness

⚡️ 🇮🇱 🇺🇸 🇮🇷 Israeli Channel 13: Israel is preparing for strikes on Iranian energy facilities, awaiting a green light from the United States. The strikes could come as early as next week, following the expiry of U.S. President Trump’s ultimatum to Iran. A senior cabinet discussion was held yesterday to approve additional targets, and the full security cabinet is expected to meet tomorrow. The last cabinet meeting included a sharp warning from the IDF chief of staff. Israel assesses that the green light will be given if the ultimatum expires and negotiations collapse. @wfwitness

🇮🇳 🇮🇷 India has bought Iranian oil for the first time since 2019. The purchase of 600,000 barrels loaded was at Kharg Island, and paid for in Indian Rupees. It is arriving at Gujarat's Vadinar refinery today. The purchase was made possible by a narrow 30-day US sanctions waiver expiring April 19. India also took delivery of 44,000 tons of Iranian LPG at Mangalore this week, loaded on a sanctioned vessel. At its peak, Iran supplied 16% of India's crude. The US "maximum pressure" campaign killed that in 2019. Now the same US is quietly reopening the door because the Strait of Hormuz closure is hitting the global economy too hard to ignore. 🔴 @DDGeopolitics
NEW: India has bought Iranian oil for the first time since 2019. The U.S. temporarily eased sanctions, allowing the purchase.
Netanyahu on Iran: After we destroyed 70% of their steel production capacity, today we attacked their petrochemical plants. We will continue to crush them.

⚡️ 🇱🇧 🇱🇧 🇮🇱 Hezbollah’s deputy political chief Mahmoud Qamati claimed the group has successfully repelled Israeli aggression despite the deployment of over 100,000 Israeli soldiers backed by tanks and aircraft. He said Israeli forces have not been able to hold any point inside Lebanese territory, and that around 150 Israeli tanks have been destroyed or disabled. Qamati stated that Hezbollah’s flexible tactics, based on urban warfare and hit‑and‑run operations, are raising attrition costs for the IDF. He added that the group has prepared for a long war and will accept no agreement without a full Israeli withdrawal from Lebanese land. @wfwitness

↩️ Reply to DD Geopolitics: "🇱🇧 IDF announces the death of Staff Sergeant Guy Ludar, age 21 in Southern Lebanon. 🔴 @DDGeopolitics | Socials | Donate | Advertising" 🇮🇱 Staff Sergeant Guy Lodar was killed in a friendly fire incident, according to Israeli Channel N12: The event occurred around 3:00 a.m. A commando force from the Maglan unit, operating under the command of Division 210, crossed the border to carry out a targeted mission in the village of Shebaa. This is an area with a predominantly Sunni population, where the IDF conducts focused operations from time to time. The objective of the operation was to arrest a militant linked to the Hezbollah terrorist organization. According to intelligence information, the suspect who was apprehended was responsible for maintaining ongoing coordination between Hezbollah and the Shebaa sector. During the operation, the force surrounded the building where the target was located and acted according to “arrest procedure,” at the end of which the suspect was successfully detained. However, during the activity, a friendly fire incident developed. While operating in the area, one of the soldiers identified figures who appeared to be fleeing the arrest scene and opened fire at them to prevent their escape, without realizing they were members of his own unit. As a result of the shooting, two soldiers from the force who were in the line of fire were hit: Lodar (of blessed memory) was struck and killed on the spot, and another soldier was seriously wounded. The IDF states that the incident is currently under a thorough investigation by operational echelons in Division 210 and the commando unit, with the aim of understanding the circumstances of the misidentification. 🔴 @DDGeopolitics

⚡ 🇩🇪 Euronews: Germany has introduced a new rule requiring men aged 17 to 45 to obtain approval before traveling abroad for more than three months under updated military service laws. The measure is part of broader reforms aimed at strengthening the Bundeswehr, including improved personnel tracking and preparedness in case of future mobilization. @wfwitness

Renowned Political Economist Prof. Carlos Lopes We certainly have manifestations of agency. South Africa taking Israel to ICC, preparations for the ports that are going to be much more in demand, Ethiopian Airlines replacing Gulf air traffic amid crisis by increasing their footprint. But the most important manifestation has been the continental free trade area, modernization of tariffs… close to 90% indicates Africans are serious. Using Africa's savings for Africa's development is now a priority for many, which is very significant. The problem is, 80% of African saving is deposited outside the continent. If we repair that we won’t need as much external money as some believe. Africa Development Bank, together with others, is putting together a new African financial architecture, which is very optimistic. FULL INTERVIEW

Türkiye's Skydagger unveiled HUNTER, Türkiye's first interceptor (anti-drone) drone, now entering serial production. Fast (up to 320 km/h) and low-cost. Designed to ram and destroy enemy drones. Uses AI + thermal/day cameras for tracking. Purpose: Replace expensive missile-based air defense with a cheap, scalable drone hunter.

The US is deploying most of its long-range JASSM-ER stealth missiles for the Iran war, pulling them from global stockpiles. Only about 425 remain available worldwide out of 2,300 prewar. These missiles have been heavily used—over 1,000 in the first month—reducing reserves meant for other conflicts. Replacing them could take years. Source: Bloomberg

↩️ Reply to WarFront Witness ⚡️ 🇮🇱 🇱🇧 A friendly fire incident in the southern Lebanese village of Shebaa (Mount Dov area) has left a Maglan unit soldier dead and another seriously wounded. The force was operating under the 210th Division around 3am, attempting to arrest a alleged Hezbollah collaborator who had been passing information on IDF activities. A soldier spotted what he believed were fleeing suspects and opened fire according to protocol for “escapees”. The figures hit were fellow Maglan fighters. One was killed, the other seriously wounded. The intended target was captured. The IDF has opened a full investigation. @wfwitness
From the WFW team, we wish all those celebrating a peaceful and blessed Easter 🐣 . May this day bring you warmth, hope, and a quiet sense of renewal, wherever you are. @wfwitness
